title: AGENTS.md
text: repository designed friendly human contributors coding agents.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Ravel serious open-source R package, thin chat wrapper. Changes improve one traits: RStudio-native workflow quality statistics-aware reasoning safe auditable actions honest provider/auth boundaries extensibility future providers tools

title: AGENTS.md
text: Keep provider support honest. Never imply consumer subscription exposes official API . Prefer official SDKs, official HTTP APIs, official CLIs reverse-engineered integrations. Default safe execution. Code previewed run applied files. Preserve structured logs actions taken. extending context collection, prefer summarized signals dumping entire objects files. Statistical helpers favor conservative, reproducible language overconfident claims.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/provider_interface.R: provider registry common contract R/auth.R: auth modes, secrets, login/logout helpers R/context_*.R: context ingestion session, objects, console, plots R/execution.R R/approvals.R: staged actions, previews, approvals, execution R/ui_gadget.R: RStudio addin chat UI R/tools_models.R: statistics-aware helpers R/tools_quarto.R: reproducible writing helpers tests/testthat/: behavior-focused unit tests

title: AGENTS.md
text: Avoid provider-specific logic UI layer. Add tests new context summarizers action helpers. Keep prompts small structured; send summarized context, raw workspace dumps. Treat RStudio APIs optional degrade cleanly outside IDE. Prefer additive provider adapters hard-coding one backend product.

title: AGENTS.md
text: change keep auth support accurate? preserve improve safety defaults? help R users real analysis work? avoid hidden execution hidden file edits? behavior logged explainable action taken?

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Ravel built around five principles: R-first context, generic text chat honest provider support auth messaging safe execution explicit approval reproducible, logged actions extensible adapters providers tools

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Files: R/addin_chat.R R/addin_settings.R R/ui_gadget.R Responsibilities: launch RStudio gadget provide first-run setup assistant live readiness checks let users choose provider model expose context toggles display chat history staged actions route user requests orchestration layer

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Files: R/provider_interface.R R/history.R Responsibilities: normalize provider discovery build message payloads chat + context store chat history action records turn provider responses user-visible messages staged actions

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Files: R/auth.R Responsibilities: store provider defaults non-sensitive settings resolve secrets environment, optional keyring, session cache report supported auth modes per provider expose honest login/logout flows surface doctor/setup information official docs key-console links

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Files: R/context_git.R R/context_session.R R/context_objects.R R/context_console.R R/context_plot.R R/tools_files.R Responsibilities: collect active document selection state summarize loaded objects type-aware compression collect project package metadata collect git branch, file status, compact diff excerpts summarize plot/device state available capture console output generated Ravel-managed execution

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Files: R/tools_models.R R/tools_quarto.R R/execution.R R/approvals.R Responsibilities: summarize interpret model objects suggest diagnostics common statistical checks draft Quarto R Markdown sections preview, approve, run code stage apply file edits

title: Ravel Architecture
text: provider adapter returns normalized provider object : name label auth_modes default_model supports_model_selection is_available() auth_status() chat(messages, context, settings) keeps UI orchestration layers provider-agnostic.

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Ravel supports four auth categories: API key OAuth bearer token CLI login state unsupported unavailable Provider honesty matters symmetry: OpenAI API: API key OpenAI Codex CLI: official sign-API key, CLI boundary GitHub Copilot: CLI login state GitHub CLI / Copilot CLI, private endpoints Gemini: API key bearer token slot Anthropic: API key

title: Ravel Architecture
text: normalized context payload contains: active document path selected code truncated active file contents project root project file listing git branch, changed files, focused diff excerpts package/session info object summaries recent Ravel-managed console output current plot metadata Context summarized sent provider. Large objects compressed schema, dimensions, classes, model statistics, small preview.

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Provider responses stay text Ravel extracts explicit action candidate. Action types: run_code insert_into_document write_file append_file draft_quarto Actions move : proposed previewed approved rejected executed logged Nothing auto-applied default MVP.

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Ravel keeps history session memory default. contributor user explicitly configures options(ravel.user_dirs = list(data = "<path>")), Ravel can mirror logs disk JSON Lines. Logs include: timestamp provider model prompt summary hash action metadata execution outcome captured console output Ravel-run actions

title: Ravel Architecture
text: Public RStudio APIs expose every aspect console plot state, native console capture partial. GitHub Copilot integration limited official CLI surfaces. ChatGPT subscription access treated general-purpose API. Gemini OAuth support varies runtime deployment context; abstraction present even API-key mode simpler path.

title: Ravel Architecture
text: MVP successful users can: open RStudio chat addin choose provider authenticate least one official path ask questions selected code loaded objects receive R-focused stats-aware responses preview generated R code running draft Quarto section model data context inspect persistent action history

title: Code of Conduct
text: Ravel aims welcoming, respectful, constructive open-source project. Contributors, maintainers, community members able participate without harassment hostility.

title: Code of Conduct
text: respectful, patient, generous feedback. Assume good intent still honest technical risks. Focus criticism ideas, code, documentation, behavior, people. Help keep provider/auth claims accurate avoid overstating unsupported features. Prefer safe, reproducible workflows discussing analytics code execution.

title: Code of Conduct
text: Harassment, intimidation, personal attacks. Discriminatory language exclusionary conduct. Publishing people’s private information without permission. Deliberately misleading users provider support, pricing, auth boundaries.

title: Code of Conduct
text: experience witness behavior violates code conduct, email Markuss Saule markusstomas@gmail.com.

title: Code of Conduct
text: Project maintainers may remove comments, reject contributions, restrict participation needed protect community project.

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: Thanks helping build Ravel.

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: Ravel generic chat wrapper. Contributions improve least one qualities: RStudio-native workflow quality statistics-aware reasoning safe auditable actions honest provider/auth boundaries extensibility future providers tools

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: development repository root:
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pak(c(   "devtools",   "lintr",   "pkgdown",   "rcmdcheck",   "testthat" )) pak::pak("msaule/ravel") devtools::load_all(".") devtools::test() lintr::lint_package()

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: Please run:

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: Prefer official SDKs, HTTP APIs, official CLIs. imply consumer subscription exposes API . Keep login-first API-key support explicitly separated docs code.

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: Favor explicit previews execution file edits. Keep chat, staged actions, context basis visually distinct. Degrade cleanly RStudio APIs unavailable.

title: Contributing to Ravel
text: Use conservative language. Call assumptions limitations matter. Prefer summaries raw dumps large objects.

title: Releasing Ravel
text: file release path GitHub, R-universe, CRAN.

title: Releasing Ravel
text: package root: Update files public release: NEWS.md README.md cran-comments.md

title: Releasing Ravel
text: R-universe’s official setup documentation says need: GitHub repository named <username>.r-universe.dev packages.json registry repository R-universe GitHub app installed account project, target registry repository : https://github.com/msaule/msaule.r-universe.dev Minimal packages.json: first successful build, add install instructions badges R-universe dashboard README.md.

title: Releasing Ravel
text: CRAN submission: Confirm R CMD check ---cran clean locally. Re-read CRAN Repository Policy submission checklist. Make sure DESCRIPTION Authors@R accurate complete. Update cran-comments.md current platforms check results. Submit devtools::release() ready.

title: Releasing Ravel
text: Revalidate live provider support release auth behavior changed. Keep provider claims conservative official. advertise consumer-login support providers expose API-key auth.

title: Ravel MVP Walkthrough
text: Ravel RStudio-native analytics copilot R users. MVP focuses : context-aware chat orchestration provider abstraction safe code staging execution model interpretation helpers Quarto drafting helpers

title: Ravel MVP Walkthrough
text: provider layer keeps auth capability boundaries explicit. OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic can run official HTTP APIs. GitHub Copilot kept behind official gh copilot CLI surface available.
code: ravel::ravel_list_providers() #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   provider  label          auth_modes default_model            #>   <chr>     <chr>          <I<list>>  <chr>                    #> 1 openai    OpenAI         <chr [2]>  gpt-5.3-codex            #> 2 copilot   GitHub Copilot <chr [2]>  copilot-cli              #> 3 gemini    Gemini         <chr [2]>  gemini-2.5-pro           #> 4 anthropic Anthropic      <chr [1]>  claude-sonnet-4-20250514

title: Ravel MVP Walkthrough
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt * am, data = mtcars) ravel::ravel_summarize_model(fit) #> $name #> [1] "fit" #>  #> $class #> [1] "lm" #>  #> $formula #> [1] "mpg ~ wt * am" #>  #> $fit #> $fit$n #> [1] 32 #>  #> $fit$aic #> [1] 157.476 #>  #> $fit$bic #> [1] 164.8046 #>  #> $fit$r_squared #> [1] 0.8330375 #>  #> $fit$adj_r_squared #> [1] 0.8151486 #>  #> $fit$sigma #> [1] 2.591247 #>  #>  #> $coefficients #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   term        estimate std_error statistic  p_value #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)    31.4      3.02      10.4  4.00e-11 #> 2 wt             -3.79     0.786     -4.82 4.55e- 5 #> 3 am             14.9      4.26       3.49 1.62e- 3 #> 4 wt:am          -5.30     1.44      -3.67 1.02e- 3 cat(ravel::ravel_interpret_model(fit)) #> Model type: lm. #> Formula: mpg ~ wt * am. #> The model explains about 83.3% of the observed variance (R-squared = 0.833). #> Term `am` has a positive association (estimate = 14.878) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt:am` has a negative association (estimate = -5.298) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt` has a negative association (estimate = -3.786) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Interaction terms are present (wt:am), so main effects should be interpreted conditionally. ravel::ravel_suggest_diagnostics(fit) #> [1] "Inspect residuals versus fitted values for non-linearity and heteroskedasticity."    #> [2] "Review a Q-Q plot of residuals for heavy tails or strong departures from normality." #> [3] "Check leverage and Cook's distance for influential observations."                    #> [4] "Consider multicollinearity diagnostics if predictors are strongly related."

title: Ravel MVP Walkthrough
code: action <- ravel::ravel_preview_code("summary(mtcars$mpg)") approved <- ravel::ravel_approve_action(action) ravel::ravel_run_code(approved) #> $success #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $output #> [1] "   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. " #> [2] "  10.40   15.43   19.20   20.09   22.80   33.90 " #>  #> $warnings #> character(0) #>  #> $messages #> character(0) #>  #> $value #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   10.40   15.43   19.20   20.09   22.80   33.90  #>  #> $error #> NULL

title: Ravel MVP Walkthrough
code: cat(ravel::ravel_draft_quarto_section("diagnostics", model = fit)) #> ## Diagnostics #>  #> ```{r diagnostics} #> # Produce diagnostic plots or checks here #> ``` #>  #> Model type: lm. #> Formula: mpg ~ wt * am. #> The model explains about 83.3% of the observed variance (R-squared = 0.833). #> Term `am` has a positive association (estimate = 14.878) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt:am` has a negative association (estimate = -5.298) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt` has a negative association (estimate = -3.786) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Interaction terms are present (wt:am), so main effects should be interpreted conditionally. #>  #> Suggested checks: #> - Inspect residuals versus fitted values for non-linearity and heteroskedasticity. #> - Review a Q-Q plot of residuals for heavy tails or strong departures from normality. #> - Check leverage and Cook's distance for influential observations. #> - Consider multicollinearity diagnostics if predictors are strongly related.

title: Ravel Showcase Workflows
text: Ravel meant feel closer analysis copilot generic chat box. point just answer question R syntax. point reason current script, selected code, loaded objects, models already memory, surrounding project, reporting workflow.

what: Workflow 1: diagnose a model that technically runs but feels wrong
title: Ravel Showcase Workflows
text: Imagine just fit model output looks suspicious. Coefficients huge, standard errors unstable, interaction terms hard explain. Ravel designed prompts like: Explain model plain English, tell interaction changes interpretation main effects, list next diagnostics run trust .
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt * am, data = mtcars) ravel::ravel_summarize_model(fit) #> $name #> [1] "fit" #>  #> $class #> [1] "lm" #>  #> $formula #> [1] "mpg ~ wt * am" #>  #> $fit #> $fit$n #> [1] 32 #>  #> $fit$aic #> [1] 157.476 #>  #> $fit$bic #> [1] 164.8046 #>  #> $fit$r_squared #> [1] 0.8330375 #>  #> $fit$adj_r_squared #> [1] 0.8151486 #>  #> $fit$sigma #> [1] 2.591247 #>  #>  #> $coefficients #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   term        estimate std_error statistic  p_value #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)    31.4      3.02      10.4  4.00e-11 #> 2 wt             -3.79     0.786     -4.82 4.55e- 5 #> 3 am             14.9      4.26       3.49 1.62e- 3 #> 4 wt:am          -5.30     1.44      -3.67 1.02e- 3 cat(ravel::ravel_interpret_model(fit)) #> Model type: lm. #> Formula: mpg ~ wt * am. #> The model explains about 83.3% of the observed variance (R-squared = 0.833). #> Term `am` has a positive association (estimate = 14.878) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt:am` has a negative association (estimate = -5.298) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt` has a negative association (estimate = -3.786) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Interaction terms are present (wt:am), so main effects should be interpreted conditionally. ravel::ravel_suggest_diagnostics(fit) #> [1] "Inspect residuals versus fitted values for non-linearity and heteroskedasticity."    #> [2] "Review a Q-Q plot of residuals for heavy tails or strong departures from normality." #> [3] "Check leverage and Cook's distance for influential observations."                    #> [4] "Consider multicollinearity diagnostics if predictors are strongly related."

what: Workflow 2: turn analysis output into Quarto writing
title: Ravel Showcase Workflows
text: already fitted object, next bottleneck often writing. Ravel can help scaffold methods, results, diagnostics sections without leaving IDE. diagnostics-heavy turn: Draft Quarto diagnostics section model, summarize main caveats, include chunk checks run next.
code: cat(ravel::ravel_draft_quarto_section("results", model = fit)) #> ## Results #>  #> ```{r results} #> # Fit or summarize the final model here #> ``` #>  #> Model type: lm. #> Formula: mpg ~ wt * am. #> The model explains about 83.3% of the observed variance (R-squared = 0.833). #> Term `am` has a positive association (estimate = 14.878) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt:am` has a negative association (estimate = -5.298) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt` has a negative association (estimate = -3.786) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Interaction terms are present (wt:am), so main effects should be interpreted conditionally. cat(ravel::ravel_draft_quarto_section("diagnostics", model = fit)) #> ## Diagnostics #>  #> ```{r diagnostics} #> # Produce diagnostic plots or checks here #> ``` #>  #> Model type: lm. #> Formula: mpg ~ wt * am. #> The model explains about 83.3% of the observed variance (R-squared = 0.833). #> Term `am` has a positive association (estimate = 14.878) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt:am` has a negative association (estimate = -5.298) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Term `wt` has a negative association (estimate = -3.786) with statistical evidence at the 0.05 level. #> Interaction terms are present (wt:am), so main effects should be interpreted conditionally. #>  #> Suggested checks: #> - Inspect residuals versus fitted values for non-linearity and heteroskedasticity. #> - Review a Q-Q plot of residuals for heavy tails or strong departures from normality. #> - Check leverage and Cook's distance for influential observations. #> - Consider multicollinearity diagnostics if predictors are strongly related.

what: Workflow 3: ask for a safe code change instead of blindly running it
title: Ravel Showcase Workflows
text: Ravel stages actions execution, matters analysis work small change can silently alter results. can approve run explicitly:
code: action <- ravel::ravel_preview_code(" summary(mtcars$mpg) quantile(mtcars$mpg) ") action$label #> [1] "Run generated R code" approved <- ravel::ravel_approve_action(action) ravel::ravel_run_code(approved) #> $success #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $output #> [1] "   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. " #> [2] "  10.40   15.43   19.20   20.09   22.80   33.90 " #> [3] "    0%    25%    50%    75%   100% "              #> [4] "10.400 15.425 19.200 22.800 33.900 "              #>  #> $warnings #> character(0) #>  #> $messages #> character(0) #>  #> $value #>     0%    25%    50%    75%   100%  #> 10.400 15.425 19.200 22.800 33.900  #>  #> $error #> NULL

what: Workflow 4: use object-aware context instead of re-explaining everything
title: Ravel Showcase Workflows
text: Ravel inspects current session, can summarize data frames, models, formulas, objects directly. enables prompts like: Summarize objects loaded, tell one looks like main modeling artifact, propose next analysis step.
code:
analysis_objects <- list(   data = mtcars,   formula = mpg ~ wt * am,   model = fit )  lapply(analysis_objects, ravel::ravel_summarize_object) #> $data #> $data$name #> [1] "X[[i]]" #>  #> $data$kind #> [1] "data.frame" #>  #> $data$class #> [1] "data.frame" #>  #> $data$rows #> [1] 32 #>  #> $data$cols #> [1] 11 #>  #> $data$columns #>  [1] "mpg"  "cyl"  "disp" "hp"   "drat" "wt"   "qsec" "vs"   "am"   "gear" #> [11] "carb" #>  #> $data$column_types #>  [1] "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" #>  [8] "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" "numeric" #>  #> $data$preview #> [1] "               mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb
Mazda RX4     21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4
Mazda RX4 Wag 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4
Datsun 710    22.8   4  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1    4    1" #>  #>  #> $formula #> $formula$name #> [1] "X[[i]]" #>  #> $formula$kind #> [1] "formula" #>  #> $formula$class #> [1] "formula" #>  #> $formula$formula #> [1] "mpg ~ wt * am" #>  #>  #> $model #> $model$name #> [1] "X[[i]]" #>  #> $model$class #> [1] "lm" #>  #> $model$formula #> [1] "mpg ~ wt * am" #>  #> $model$fit #> $model$fit$n #> [1] 32 #>  #> $model$fit$aic #> [1] 157.476 #>  #> $model$fit$bic #> [1] 164.8046 #>  #> $model$fit$r_squared #> [1] 0.8330375 #>  #> $model$fit$adj_r_squared #> [1] 0.8151486 #>  #> $model$fit$sigma #> [1] 2.591247 #>  #>  #> $model$coefficients #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   term        estimate std_error statistic  p_value #>   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)    31.4      3.02      10.4  4.00e-11 #> 2 wt             -3.79     0.786     -4.82 4.55e- 5 #> 3 am             14.9      4.26       3.49 1.62e- 3 #> 4 wt:am          -5.30     1.44      -3.67 1.02e- 3 #>  #> $model$kind #> [1] "model"

what: Workflow 5: review analytical code, not just syntax

Summarize latest analysis diff tell validate merge . Convert selected tidyverse pipeline base R, keep readable consistent rest file. exactly kinds tasks project-aware assistant feels different plain browser chat.

code: install.packages("ravel") library(ravel) ravel::ravel_setup_addin() if (!requireNamespace("pak", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pak") pak::pak("msaule/ravel") library(ravel) ravel::ravel_setup_addin()

title: Ravel
text: Install ravel CRAN. Run ravel::ravel_setup_addin() connect least one provider. Run ravel::ravel_chat_addin() RStudio open chat UI.

title: Ravel
text: Context-aware default. Ravel gathers active editor, selected code, workspace objects, console state captured Ravel actions, project files, recent git diffs responds. Built statistical work. explains lm() glm() results, coefficients, interactions, fit diagnostics, common modeling pitfalls plain English. Safe acts. Generated code previewed, file edits staged, actions logged instead silently executed. Designed RStudio. setup flow, chat UI, action workflow live inside RStudio addins rather treating R thin wrapper around generic chat window. Multi-provider without pretending. Ravel supports official APIs official CLIs , clear messaging provider auth path unavailable.

title: Ravel
text: kinds prompts Ravel starts feeling different normal chat window. Ravel sees selected model code, fitted object memory, latest console output. Useful model technically runs analysis feels wrong. Ravel can use active selection, nearby script context, project files rewrite matches rest analysis written. Good teaching, package work, mixed-style codebases. model object memory, Ravel can help draft prose, interpretation, chunk scaffolding matches current analysis. Especially strong already lm() glm() objects loaded. Ravel reads workspace git state recent diffs, helps reason whether change cosmetic, risky, statistically meaningful. Helpful want reviewer understands code analysis intent. Ravel can inspect model summaries object structure, translate coefficient tables plain language actually usable. Ideal teaching, presentations, cleaning methods/results language. Instead pasting fragments browser tab, can keep active script, objects, recent output attached conversation. RStudio-native workflow matters .
code: Why do these logistic coefficients explode? Check whether separation is plausible, tell me what diagnostics to run next, and draft a Quarto diagnostics subsection. Convert this dplyr pipeline to base R, keep the same output shape, and explain each transformation step. Write a results section from this model, explain the interaction in plain English, and include a Quarto chunk for follow-up plots. Summarize what changed in this analysis, tell me which edits could change results, and suggest a validation checklist before merge. Explain these coefficients like I have to present them. What is the reference group? How does the interaction change the main-effect interpretation? Why is this join failing right now? Use the selected code and loaded objects, then propose the smallest safe fix.

title: Execute a staged action — ravel_apply_action

title: ravel 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-03 Published CRAN, install.packages("ravel") now works stable release. Tightened install guidance resilient pak-first quick start. Added release-assets GitHub Actions workflow tagged releases upload source tarballs automatically. Simplified root license files repository presents canonical MIT license.

title: ravel 0.1.0
text: Initial public MVP RStudio-native analytics copilot R. Added multi-provider support OpenAI, GitHub Copilot CLI, Gemini, Anthropic. Added guided setup, auth helpers, live provider verification. Added active-editor, workspace, object, console, plot, activity, git-aware context collection. Added safe staged execution, action logging, model interpretation helpers, Quarto drafting tools.
